summary: The uptrend snapped one day before the Fed. NQ reversed -1.79% off a fresh overnight high to close 29,985.75, below its 30,302 trend stop, and ES confirmed (-0.59%), also closing below its stop, so both index trend legs broke together. This was orderly de-risking, not panic: VIX rose only 1.30% to 16.41. Gold caught the defensive bid (+0.51%, holding its 4,338 stop), while crude crashed another -5.62%. Throttle steps to DEFENSIVE into Wednesday's 2:00 PM ET FOMC decision.

setups.name: AVWAP Hand-off
setups.entry: Short continuation below AVWAP after the OR down-break
setups.bias: Down-trend day, OR broke down at 11:00 ET, hand-off below AVWAP held. Stand down into Wed 2:00 PM.

setups.name: AVWAP Deviation Band
setups.entry: Reversion only on a volume-exhaustion tag of -2s/-3s
setups.bias: Borderline: MNQ tagged -2s 29,931 at the RTH low, but on a trend day tags fail more than they revert.

event.name: FOMC eve (no scheduled release; decision Wednesday)
event.nqReaction: NQ -1.79% to 29,985.75. A fresh overnight high at 30,665.75 failed and price sold off all session to close below the 30,302 trend stop. Position-squaring into the Fed, not a news-driven move.
event.crossAsset: Broad de-risk: ES -0.59% also lost its ATR stop, VIX +1.30% to 16.41 (first up day after three down, but still low, so orderly). Gold +0.51% held its stop, the defensive rotation. Crude -5.62% extended its downtrend. Decision plus SEP and dot plot Wed Jun 17 2:00 PM ET, Chair Warsh's first.

observe.name: GAP_NQ
observe.note: No large opening gap; RTH opened near the prior close then trended down. No gap-fade setup.

observe.name: TSMOM252LO_NQ
observe.note: Higher-timeframe 252 filter can still read long, but the 1hr leg broke today. Watch for the longer filter to confirm or diverge.

risk.ddProximity: NORMAL band; trend long stopped out flat, no drawdown pressure
risk.sizing: No new index longs until stops reclaim (MNQ 30,262, MES 7,550). No knife-catch shorts into the 2:00 PM print. Gold long intact above 4,338 but small; crude a no-touch downtrend.
risk.note: FOMC decision plus SEP and dot plot Wednesday Jun 17 2:00 PM ET (Chair Warsh's first) is the dominant catalyst. The trend long (TSMOM-20) stopped out at the trail today, so open trend risk is now flat; the loss was given-back open gains, not the daily budget. The binding constraint remains event exposure. Stand down all discretionary risk into the decision and the first post-print volatility.

psychology: A stop-out is information, not an invitation to revenge-flip short into a 2:00 PM Fed print. Yesterday's hardest-long-to-hold became today's stop, exactly as the map warned, and the trail did its job. You do not owe the market a reversal trade. Size down or stand aside through Wednesday's decision, let the first volatility burst pass, and re-engage on a confirmed level rather than on the headline.

fullReport.title: Yesterday's Map: Marked (Jun 15 to Jun 16)
fullReport.html:
#Map rowResultGrade
1MNQ holds above 30,302 rising ATR stopNOT TRIGGEREDNeutral. The hold failed; price lost 30,302 on a morning hourly close.
2MNQ hourly close back below 30,302 ATR stopTRIGGEREDPLAYED OUT. Lost 30,302 by the 10:00 ET close, closed 29,985.75 (-1.79%); stop flipped short to 30,262.
3MNQ tags +2s 30,620 on a volume-exhaustion barTRIGGEREDPLAYED OUT. Overnight high 30,665.75 pushed through 30,620, then reversed to close 29,985.75, a 680-pt fade. Tag was overnight.
4MGC loses 4,325 rising stop on an hourly closeNOT TRIGGEREDNeutral. Gold held the stop, closed 4,353.3 (+0.51%) above the rising 4,338 trail.
5MCL hourly close back above 82.31 stop and 85 psychNOT TRIGGEREDNeutral. Crude fell -5.62% to 76.62; the wait-for-reclaim bias held up.
